message:
feat(cli): use bootstrap prefix resolution for info and where commands in no-db mode

When running with --no-db, the info and where commands now use
resolve_bootstrap_issue_prefix() to derive a prefix from the workspace
directory name (via abbreviate_prefix) when no JSONL file or explicit
config exists. Previously, no-db mode would fall through to None when
the JSONL was missing, leaving the prefix unresolved.

Changes:
- info.rs: refactor resolved_prefix logic into no-db vs db branches,
  calling resolve_bootstrap_issue_prefix for no-db mode
- where.rs: simplify detect_prefix by extracting effective_startup
  earlier, then delegating to resolve_bootstrap_issue_prefix when
  no_db is true instead of manually checking JSONL state
- config/mod.rs: widen resolve_bootstrap_issue_prefix visibility from
  private to pub(crate) so info and where commands can call it

Both commands gain tests verifying bootstrap prefix derivation from the
workspace directory name when --no-db is set and no JSONL exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1401,7 +1401,7 @@ pub fn no_auto_import_from_layer(layer: &ConfigLayer) -> Option<bool> {
     .and_then(|value| parse_bool(value))
 }
 
-fn resolve_bootstrap_issue_prefix(
+pub(crate) fn resolve_bootstrap_issue_prefix(
     bootstrap_layer: &ConfigLayer,
     beads_dir: &Path,
     jsonl_path: &Path,
